frame_analyses.description: Initial setup showing training mat with four circular marked targets and suture material present
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean setup with clearly marked training targets

frame_analyses.description: Left instrument grasping needle, beginning needle loading process
Continuing needle loading with left instrument positioning needle
Both instruments visible, preparing for first bite placement at upper marked target
Taking first bite through tissue at upper target, needle passing through material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate bite depth and placement technique

frame_analyses.description: Completing first bite, both suture ends visible for knot tying preparation
Beginning knot construction with right instrument manipulating suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initiating knot with right hand - appears to be starting surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Tightening first throw with both instruments pulling in opposite directions
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bilateral traction for knot security

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw with left instrument now taking lead
Completing second throw formation and beginning tightening
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth throw construction with good hand coordination

frame_analyses.description: Beginning third throw with right instrument manipulation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Continued proper alternation of hands between throws

frame_analyses.description: Final tightening of completed knot with bilateral tension
Positioning scissors for suture cutting close to knot
Task completed with secure knot and cut suture ends
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean final result with secure knot

knot_assessments.notes: Good surgeon's knot formation with double wrap visible
Proper hand alternation with secure single throw
Final throw with good alternation and secure tightening

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Good tissue approximation with no visible gaps after knot completion

technique_summary: Solid intracorporeal knot tying performance with proper surgeon's knot, good hand alternation, and secure final result. Time was somewhat prolonged but technique was sound throughout.
improvement_suggestions: Work on increasing speed while maintaining quality
Practice more efficient needle loading to reduce initial setup time
